Riverside Community Foundation

Spring 2026 Neighborhood Impact Mini-Grant — Online application preview (sample fixture for testing)

Home · Grants · Apply — Mini-Grant

Neighborhood Impact Mini-Grant

This program supports small nonprofits and community groups serving Riverside County with one-time awards of up to $7,500 for programs that strengthen youth development, food security, or civic engagement. This page mirrors a typical funder application: eligibility narrative, deadlines, and numbered narrative questions.

Application opensJanuary 15, 2026
Deadline (submitted online)March 31, 2026, 11:59 p.m. Pacific Time
Award notificationMay 15, 2026
Earliest project startJuly 1, 2026
Grant periodUp to 12 consecutive months
Before you begin: You will need your organization’s EIN, most recent fiscal year operating budget, a short description of the community need, and contact information for an authorized signatory. Draft responses offline if you prefer; there is no auto-save on this sample page.

Eligibility & requirements

Applicants must be tax-exempt under Section 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code or be a public school, faith-based organization with a fiscal sponsor, or a recognized neighborhood association with documented bylaws.

Organizations with an annual operating budget over $2 million are not eligible for this mini-grant cycle. Prior grantees may re-apply after twelve months from their last award letter date.

Application questions

Answer each question below in full sentences unless otherwise indicated. Character limits, where stated, include spaces.

  1. 1. Legal name of the organization

    Enter the name exactly as it appears on your IRS determination letter or state charter.

    Short answer (maximum 120 characters).

  2. 2. Mission statement

    In 200–600 words, describe your organization’s mission and the population you primarily serve. Reference geographic focus if applicable.

  3. 3. Primary program area for this request

    Select the single category that best describes the funded work.

    • Youth development & out-of-school time
    • Food security & nutrition access
    • Civic engagement & neighborhood leadership
    • Environmental justice tied to community health
    • Other (explain in question 7)
  4. 4. Counties served

    List all California counties where participants will receive direct services from this grant. Use a comma-separated list.

  5. 5. Total annual operating budget (most recently completed fiscal year)

    Enter a whole-dollar amount, numbers only (no symbols). Example: 485000

  6. 6. Fiscal sponsor relationship

    Does your organization use a fiscal sponsor for this application?

    • Yes — we will submit under a 501(c)(3) fiscal sponsor
    • No — we apply under our own tax-exempt status

    If Yes, attach sponsor’s letter of commitment (not collected on this sample page).

  7. 7. Project narrative

    Describe the proposed activities, timeline, partners, and how you will measure success. You may use up to 1,800 words.

    Address: (a) community need with local data or observation; (b) roles of staff and volunteers; (c) budget alignment with outcomes.

  8. 8. Anticipated project start date

    Provide a date on or after July 1, 2026, in ISO format: YYYY-MM-DD.

  9. 9. Authorized signatory

    Full name, title, email, and daytime phone for the person authorized to execute a grant agreement.

  10. 10. Equity practices

    Which practices does your organization currently use? Check all that apply (list the letters in your answer, e.g. A, C, D).

    • A — Bilingual or multilingual front-line services
    • B — Board or advisory group reflects community demographics
    • C — Paid staff compensation reviewed for parity at least biennially
    • D — Community members co-design program changes
    • E — None of the above yet; describe planned steps in question 7

Compliance & certifications

By submitting an application, the authorized signatory certifies that the organization is not debarred from federal or state funding, will use funds only for charitable purposes described herein, and will complete a final report within 45 days of the grant end date.

Discrimination on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, marital status, sexual orientation, or gender identity in the funded program is prohibited.

Sample fixture for local testing only — not affiliated with any real foundation.
© Riverside Community Foundation (fictitious) — Spring 2026
